data.text: I’ll use the HELIX/DDx execution instructions for this bead and start by reading the named Svelte page, existing action/worker patterns, and the e2e test layout before editing.

The worktree already has unrelated skill-link changes outside this bead; I’ll leave those alone and stage only the frontend/test/snapshot files I intentionally touch. The Svelte path is under `cli/internal/server/frontend`, so I’m switching into that package context now.

The project overview currently redirects straight to `/beads`, so the feature needs to turn that route into a real overview page. I’m checking the GraphQL/client conventions and route data shape so the new panel fits existing patterns.

I found the existing FEAT-008 GraphQL contract: `workerDispatch` accepts `execute-loop`, `realign-specs`, and `run-checks`, and there are already shared `ConfirmDialog` and `Tooltip` primitives. I’ll use those directly and keep the new page scoped to the project overview route plus its screenshot test entry.

I’m going to replace the redirect page with a real project overview, add a page load for `queueSummary`, make the shared tooltip declare `role="tooltip"` explicitly, and add the project overview to the screenshot matrix so its light/dark baselines can be generated.

The first pass is in place: the overview now owns `queueSummary`, opens the shared confirmation dialog per action, dispatches the existing worker mutation, and swaps successful actions to worker-detail links. I’m running the frontend checks now to catch Svelte/TypeScript issues before browser tests.

`bun run check` failed immediately because frontend dependencies are not installed in this worktree (`svelte-kit` is missing), not because of the code yet. I’m going to install the frontend package with the existing lockfile, then rerun checks and the targeted e2e.

Svelte-check found one real issue: I passed a parameterized snippet where the tooltip expects a zero-argument snippet. I’m simplifying that to the component’s string `content` prop and adding a Svelte ignore for the intentionally explicit `section role="region"` required by the bead.
Type checking is green for the touched code; the remaining warnings are pre-existing in unrelated components. Next I’m running the required `e2e/actions.spec.ts` suite against the built preview.

The targeted Playwright run is blocked by an existing process on the configured preview port `4173`. I’m checking what owns that port so I can clear only the stale frontend server and rerun the suite.

The e2e run reached the page but did not render the panel, then the preview server exited, so I’m reading the Playwright error context and reproducing the route in a narrower run to identify whether this is a route/data issue or a build-preview crash.

The first failure is contaminated by another preview server on `4173` from a parallel worktree; its UI even has a nav item this worktree does not. I’m making the frontend Playwright port configurable for verification isolation, and I’m also fixing the dialog close icon label so it doesn’t collide with the `/confirm|start/` matcher.
The dialog and test-port fixes type-check cleanly. I’m rerunning the required actions suite on an isolated port so this worktree is not using another execution’s preview server.

The disabled branch exposed a runtime issue in the shared tooltip primitive: Bits UI needs a `Tooltip.Provider`, and the component had only been import-tested before. I’m wrapping the primitive internally so disabled action tooltips can render wherever the component is used.
The tooltip runtime fix type-checks. I stopped the manual preview server and am rerunning `actions.spec.ts` on the isolated port.

The final action test now finds the disabled button, but the wrapper was intercepting Playwright’s hover. I’m adjusting the `Tooltip` primitive’s `disabledTrigger` mode to use a non-intercepting span/CSS tooltip, which preserves the required span-wrapped disabled button and exposes `role="tooltip"` on hover.
`e2e/actions.spec.ts` is now 5/5 green on the isolated port. I’m generating the required light and dark project overview screenshot baselines next, scoped to the new project overview tests only.

The screenshot run updated the tracked static `build/index.html` hash references as a side effect. That file is generated output unrelated to this bead’s deliverable, so I’m putting it back and leaving only the source, test, and snapshot changes.

The generated build hash diff is gone. I have the required actions suite and screenshot update passing; I’m running the frontend unit suite as an extra guard for the shared primitive changes before staging.

The generated baselines showed the disabled tooltip because static-preview fallback data reports zero ready beads. I’m changing only the static preview fallback to provide a small ready queue so the committed overview baselines show the normal Actions panel state; the disabled state remains covered by the e2e mock.
The fallback tweak is check/unit clean. I’m rerunning the required actions suite and regenerating the project overview baselines once more so the final verification reflects the committed state.

The live preview now renders the normal fallback state (`3 ready`, `1 blocked`), but Playwright kept the earlier snapshots because the pixel delta stayed under tolerance. I’m deleting the two generated project overview baselines and recreating them so the committed PNGs match the current overview state.
The live preview and generated PNGs look correct now, and no preview servers are left running. I’m doing a focused formatting check on the files I touched before staging.
Prettier adjusted the screenshot spec and tooltip component. I’m rerunning the check and the required actions e2e after formatting so the final state is verified, not just the pre-format state.
Post-format checks are green and `actions.spec.ts` is still 5/5. I’m doing one final project-overview screenshot comparison without updating snapshots, then I’ll stage the scoped files and commit.

Formatting is clean, the generated build file is back to its original state, and only the intended frontend files plus the two project overview snapshots remain. I’m staging those explicit paths and committing with the bead ID.

Implemented and committed the bead work.

Commit: `fb416995 feat: add project actions panel [ddx-c825fd8b]`

Verified:
- `bun run check` passed
- `bun run test:unit -- --run` passed, 8/8
- `PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=4193 bunx playwright test e2e/actions.spec.ts --workers=1` passed, 5/5
- `PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=4193 bunx playwright test e2e/screenshots.spec.ts -g 'project-overview' --workers=1` passed, 2/2

Light and dark project overview screenshot baselines are committed. Unrelated pre-existing skill-link worktree changes were left untouched.
